steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the overall structure of the passage. Map out what each part does: the first sentence introduces something all baleen whales share (filtering krill through baleen plates). The passage then shifts to comparing humpback and fin whale sizes, which is relevant to how much krill each can consume. The correct answer must capture both parts in the right order.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It draws a distinction between the sizes of the humpback whale and the fin whale and then presents a feature that they have in common.: the order is reversed — the shared feature (baleen feeding) comes first, the size difference comes second. ✗
  • Choice B — It details a relationship between humpback and fin whales and krill and then discusses an unexpected consequence of a change in baleen whale populations.: no change in populations is mentioned anywhere in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — It discusses a unique feature of the humpback whale and how the lack of that feature in the fin whale explains differences in the two species’ behavior.: baleen feeding is not unique to the humpback — the passage explicitly says all baleen whales share it. ✗
  • Choice D — It describes a characteristic shared by all baleen whales and then illustrates a difference between the humpback whale and the fin whale that is relevant to that characteristic.: the shared characteristic (baleen feeding) is introduced first, then the size difference between humpback and fin whale is shown to matter because size affects how much krill each can consume. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
